name: Type Real Code
concept: Real JavaScript — no blocks, you type the code yourself

learn: The blocks come off. From here you TYPE the code — like every real programmer does
You already know all of it! move, loops, ifs, functions, variables — same ideas, now typed
Every instruction ends with a semicolon ; and every { must have a matching }
JavaScript is picky about CAPITAL letters: moveForward() works, moveforward() does not
You WILL get errors. Good! Errors are the computer telling you exactly what to fix — read them and try again

blocks.name: the code box
blocks.text: type your program here. Press ▶ Run to see the robot follow it

blocks.name: the snippet buttons
blocks.text: tap one to drop that code in — no hunting for ( ) { } ; on the keyboard!

blocks.name: the error line
blocks.text: if something's wrong, the robot tells you what and where. That's debugging — every coder does it all day

blocks.name: reset
blocks.text: puts the starter code back if you get stuck

intro.goal: Same puzzles you've mastered — but now the program comes out of your own fingers.

recap: You wrote real JavaScript by hand — the same language that runs every website on Earth
You typed all five fundamentals: order, for loops, if/else, functions, and variables
You learned the rules that trip up every beginner: semicolons, matching braces, and CAPITAL letters
You read error messages and fixed your own code — that's what real programming actually feels like
Nothing here was pretend. You can type this exact code into any browser's console today

quiz.why: JavaScript is case-sensitive: capital F, and () to call it, and ; to end the line.

quiz.q: What does the ; at the end of a line mean?
quiz.why: The semicolon ends a statement — like a period at the end of a sentence.

quiz.q: You typed a { but forgot the matching }. What happens?
choices: an error — the computer never sees the end of the block
quiz.why: Every { needs a }. The computer can't guess where your block ends.

quiz.q: for (var i = 0; i < 5; i++) { moveForward(); } — how far does the robot walk?
quiz.why: That's World 2's loop, typed for real: it runs the line inside 5 times.

quiz.q: if (onGem()) { collectGem(); } — when does collectGem() run?
choices: only when the robot is standing on a gem
quiz.why: World 3, typed: the code inside { } runs only when the question is true.

quiz.q: function star() { moveForward(); } — what happens when the robot reaches this line?
choices: nothing yet — it just learns the move
quiz.why: World 4 rule: defining is not running. You have to call star(); to use it.

quiz.q: var gems = 0; gems = gems + 1; — what is gems now?
quiz.why: World 5, typed: = means STORE. Take gems, add 1, store it back.

quiz.q: You get an error message. The best move is…
choices: read it — it says what's wrong and where
ignore it and press Run again
quiz.why: Errors are help, not punishment. Every professional reads them all day long.

levels.name: Your First Line
levels.starter:
// Type your program below.
// Tap the 🧱 buttons if you don't want to type it all!
levels.hint: Two lines: moveForward(); then collectGem(); — don't forget the semicolons!

levels.name: Line by Line
levels.hint: Count the squares to the gem. One moveForward(); per square, then collectGem();

levels.name: Turn It
levels.hint: Walk to the corner, turnRight();, walk down, then collectGem();

levels.name: Type a Loop
levels.hint: for (var i = 0; i < 6; i++) { moveForward(); } then collectGem(); — tap the 🔁 button to get the shape right!

levels.name: Type an If
levels.hint: Put the if INSIDE the loop: walk one step, then if (onGem()) { collectGem(); }

levels.name: Type an If-Else
levels.hint: The self-steering robot, typed: if (pathAhead()) { moveForward(); } else { turnRight(); }

levels.name: Type a Function
levels.hint: function star() { ... } teaches the move. Then star(); three times to use it.

levels.name: Type a Variable
levels.hint: Count 3 gems into the variable, then for (var i = 0; i < gems; i++) { moveForward(); } to the flag 🏁

levels.name: Put It Together
levels.hint: One loop, two ifs — steer with pathAhead(), scoop with onGem(). All typed.

levels.name: Boss: Real Programmer
levels.hint: Boss! Every tool is unlocked. Steer, scoop all 3 gems, land on the flag 🏁 — typed by your own hands.
